title: Mind Reader at the White House Dinner? Oz Pearlman Will Try to Trick Trump!
summary:
Imagine sitting in a huge room full of reporters and politicians. The President of the United States is there. And one guy on stage claims he can read minds. That's exactly what's happening at this year's White House Correspondents' Dinner!

Usually, a comedian makes jokes and roasts (makes fun of) the politicians. But this year, they picked a mentalist — someone who does magic tricks that look like mind-reading. His name is Oz Pearlman, and he's famous for guessing people's PIN numbers and secret thoughts on TV shows and social media.

Oz started doing magic as a teenager to pay for college. He even worked on Wall Street! But his big break came when he finished third on America's Got Talent in 2015. Now he's going to perform in front of President Trump.

"Reading Donald Trump's mind is arguably the most impressive thing you could ever do," Oz said. He's been planning this moment for 10 years!

Some people think it's weird to have a mentalist instead of a comedian. But a professor who studies magic says mentalism actually makes people laugh just as much. And Oz hopes his show will bring people together, even if they disagree on politics.

Will he really read the President's mind? We'll have to wait until Saturday night to find out. But one thing's for sure: it's going to be a night nobody forgets!
why_it_matters: This shows how entertainment can be used to bring people together, even when they disagree on things like politics.

background_read: Every year, the President of the United States attends a big dinner with journalists and celebrities.
Usually a comedian tells jokes about the president and politicians, but this year a magician is doing tricks.
Mentalism is like magic, but instead of objects, the performer seems to guess your thoughts.
Article_Structure: WHAT: Will perform at the White House Correspondents' Dinner
WHERE: White House dinner in Washington, D.C.
WHY: The dinner chose a mentalist instead of a comedian to entertain the audience

perspectives.description: Having a mentalist is a fun surprise that might make the dinner more interesting and memorable.
Some people think it's weird to have a mind-reading act instead of a comedian who tells political jokes.
Oz Pearlman believes his show can make people laugh and forget their differences for a while.
